title: E001: Add Keystone API types, webhooks & CRD generation
description:
**Size:** 🏗️ large
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** critical

## Decisions (from open questions)

1. **Cache.Backend defaulting** → Yes, `Default()` sets `Cache.Backend = "dogpile.cache.pymemcache"` when empty (per architecture doc line 318-319).
2. **`groupversion_info.go`** → Yes, included in scope (mandatory prerequisite — types and webhook reference `SchemeBuilder` and `GroupVersion`).
3. **`Fernet.RotationSchedule` defaulting** → Rely on Kubebuilder marker only (`+kubebuilder:default="0 0 * * 0"`); `Default()` does not set it.

## Summary

Define the Keystone CRD types (`CC-0011`) as the reference implementation for all CobaltCore service operators. This includes the root `Keystone` type with Kubebuilder markers, the full `KeystoneSpec` with validated sub-specs (Database, Cache, Fernet, Federation, Bootstrap, Middleware, Plugins, PolicyOverrides, ExtraConfig), a `KeystoneStatus` with conditions and endpoint, defaulting and validation webhooks, CRD YAML generation via controller-gen, and comprehensive unit tests for every webhook rule.

## Scope

**Included:**
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go` — Keystone, KeystoneList, KeystoneSpec, KeystoneStatus, FernetSpec, FederationSpec, BootstrapSpec with all Kubebuilder markers (`+object:root`, `+subresource:status`, `+printcolumn`, `+validation:Minimum`, `+default`, `+validation:XValidation`)
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/groupversion_info.go` — SchemeBuilder, GroupVersion (`keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/v1alpha1`), AddToScheme
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go` — `Default()` (Replicas→3, Fernet.MaxActiveKeys→3, Cache.Backend→"dogpile.cache.pymemcache", Bootstrap.AdminUser→"admin", Bootstrap.Region→"RegionOne"), `ValidateCreate()`/`ValidateUpdate()` delegating to shared `validate()` with: cron expression validation, duplicate plugin ConfigSection detection, PolicyOverrides source requirement, empty policy rule name detection
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go` — generated by `controller-gen object`
- `operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml` — generated by `controller-gen crd`
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook_test.go` — table-driven unit tests with Gomega for every defaulting and validation rule
- `operators/keystone/main.go` update — add `keystonev1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme)` in `init()`
- `/workspace/Makefile` update — wire `generate` and `manifests` targets to controller-gen for keystone operator
- `operators/keystone/go.mod` update — add `github.com/robfig/cron/v3` as direct dependency

**Excluded:**
- Reconciler implementation (CC-0012+, separate feature)
- Webhook certificate management / cert-manager integration (infrastructure concern, separate)
- `ValidateDelete()` webhook (no delete validation required per architecture docs)
- Conversion webhooks for future API versions (YAGNI — only v1alpha1 exists)
- E2E/integration tests with envtest (separate task, this covers unit tests only)
- Status condition logic / condition helpers (already implemented in `internal/common/conditions/`)
- Shared types modifications (`DatabaseSpec`, `CacheSpec`, etc. already exist in `internal/common/types/`)

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph api["operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/"]
        GV["groupversion_info.go\nSchemeBuilder, GroupVersion"]
        Types["keystone_types.go\nKeystone, KeystoneSpec, KeystoneStatus\nFernetSpec, FederationSpec, BootstrapSpec"]
        WH["keystone_webhook.go\nDefault, ValidateCreate, ValidateUpdate"]
        DC["zz_generated.deepcopy.go\n#40;generated#41;"]
        Tests["keystone_webhook_test.go\nTable-driven unit tests"]
    end

    subgraph common["internal/common/types/"]
        ST["types.go\nImageSpec, DatabaseSpec, CacheSpec\nSecretRefSpec, PolicySpec\nPluginSpec, MiddlewareSpec"]
    end

    subgraph gen["Generation"]
        CG["controller-gen object\n+ controller-gen crd"]
        CRD["config/crd/bases/\nkeystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml"]
    end

    subgraph entry["operators/keystone/"]
        Main["main.go\nScheme registration"]
    end

    Types -->|"imports commonv1"| ST
    WH -->|"validates"| Types
    GV -->|"SchemeBuilder"| Types
    CG -->|"generates"| DC
    CG -->|"generates"| CRD
    Types -->|"input"| CG
    Main -->|"AddToScheme"| GV
    Tests -->|"tests"| WH
```

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph defaulting["Default webhook - 5 fields"]
        D1["Replicas: 0 -> 3"]
        D2["Fernet.MaxActiveKeys: 0 -> 3"]
        D3["Cache.Backend: empty -> dogpile.cache.pymemcache"]
        D4["Bootstrap.AdminUser: empty -> admin"]
        D5["Bootstrap.Region: empty -> RegionOne"]
    end

    subgraph validation["Validation webhook - 5 rules"]
        V1["Replicas >= 1"]
        V2["Fernet.RotationSchedule valid cron"]
        V3["No duplicate Plugin.ConfigSection"]
        V4["PolicyOverrides: rules or configMapRef set"]
        V5["PolicyOverrides: no empty rule names"]
    end

    subgraph xvalidation["XValidation CEL - 2 rules"]
        X1["Database: clusterRef XOR host"]
        X2["PolicyOverrides: rules OR configMapRef"]
    end
```

## Key Components

- **`keystone_types.go`**: Root `Keystone` type with 3 printcolumns (Ready/Endpoint/Age), `KeystoneSpec` with 11 fields importing shared types from `commonv1`, sub-specs `FernetSpec` (cron schedule + max keys), `FederationSpec` (enabled bool), `BootstrapSpec` (admin user + secret ref + region), `KeystoneStatus` with conditions array + endpoint string, `KeystoneList` for list operations, `init()` scheme registration
- **`groupversion_info.go`**: `GroupVersion` constant (`keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/v1alpha1`), `SchemeBuilder` and `AddToScheme` var (~15 lines of boilerplate)
- **`keystone_webhook.go`**: `Default()` method (5 field defaults), `ValidateCreate()`/`ValidateUpdate()` delegating to shared `validate()` with 5 validation rules using `field.ErrorList` + `apierrors.NewInvalid()`, imports `robfig/cron/v3` for cron parsing
- **`keystone_webhook_test.go`**: Table-driven tests covering: each default applied when zero-value, defaults not overriding explicit values, valid cron accepted, invalid cron rejected, duplicate plugin sections rejected, PolicyOverrides without source rejected, empty policy rule names rejected, valid specs passing, replicas < 1 rejected
- **`zz_generated.deepcopy.go`**: Generated by `controller-gen object` — DeepCopyObject/DeepCopyInto for all types
- **CRD YAML**: Generated by `controller-gen crd` with full OpenAPI v3 schema, XValidation rules, printer columns, status subresource
- **`main.go` update**: Add `keystonev1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme)` import and registration in `init()`
- **`Makefile` update**: Wire `generate` and `manifests` targets from current no-ops to `controller-gen` commands scoped to `operators/keystone/api/...`
- **`go.mod` update**: Add `github.com/robfig/cron/v3` as direct dependency (already an indirect dep via `internal/common`)The elaboration is complete. All sections delivered:

- **Decisions**: 3 user-answered questions incorporated (Cache.Backend defaulting, groupversion_info.go inclusion, Fernet.RotationSchedule marker-only)
- **Summary**: CC-0011 reference implementation scoped
- **Scope**: 9 in-scope items, 7 excluded with YAGNI rationale
- **Visualization**: 2 Mermaid diagrams (component architecture + webhook rule breakdown)
- **Key Components**: 9 components mapped to actual codebase paths

Every aspect of the original feature request verified against the architecture doc (`03-crd-implementation.md`) and existing codebase: root type with 3 printcolumns, all 11 KeystoneSpec fields (Replicas, Image, Database, Cache, Fernet, Federation, Bootstrap, Middleware, Plugins, PolicyOverrides, ExtraConfig), XValidation CEL rules, KeystoneStatus, defaulting webhook (5 defaults), validation webhook (5 rules), CRD YAML generation, and unit tests.

<promise>IMPLEMENTATION_COMPLETE</promise>

stories.title: Platform operator creates a Keystone CR with minimal spec and gets sensible defaults
stories.want: to create a Keystone CR with only required fields and have optional fields defaulted to sensible values
stories.so_that: I can deploy Keystone quickly without specifying every configuration option
criteria: Submitting a Keystone CR with replicas=0 results in replicas=3 after defaulting
Submitting a Keystone CR with empty cache.backend results in 'dogpile.cache.pymemcache' after defaulting
Submitting a Keystone CR with empty bootstrap.adminUser results in 'admin' after defaulting
Submitting a Keystone CR with empty bootstrap.region results in 'RegionOne' after defaulting
Submitting a Keystone CR with empty fernet.maxActiveKeys results in 3 after defaulting
Explicitly set values are NOT overridden by the defaulting webhook

stories.title: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
stories.want: to receive clear, field-specific error messages when I submit an invalid Keystone CR
stories.so_that: I can correct my configuration before the operator attempts reconciliation
criteria: Submitting replicas < 1 returns a validation error referencing spec.replicas
Submitting an invalid cron expression in fernet.rotationSchedule returns an error with the parse failure
Submitting duplicate plugin configSection values returns a Duplicate field error
Submitting policyOverrides with neither rules nor configMapRef returns a Required field error
Submitting policyOverrides with an empty rule name returns an Invalid field error

stories.title: API server rejects structurally invalid Keystone CRs via OpenAPI schema and CEL rules
stories.want: the API server to reject CRs that violate structural constraints before they reach the webhook
stories.so_that: I get the fastest possible feedback on schema violations
criteria: CRD YAML includes XValidation CEL rule enforcing exactly one of database.clusterRef or database.host
CRD YAML includes XValidation CEL rule enforcing policyOverrides has rules or configMapRef
CRD YAML includes kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1 for replicas
CRD YAML includes kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=3 for fernet.maxActiveKeys
CRD YAML includes status subresource and 3 printer columns (Ready, Endpoint, Age)

stories.title: Developer creates a new service operator CRD following the Keystone reference pattern
stories.want: to use Keystone's types, webhook, and generation setup as a reference implementation
stories.so_that: all CobaltCore operators follow consistent CRD patterns
criteria: groupversion_info.go follows the standard kubebuilder GroupVersion boilerplate pattern
Types file imports shared types from internal/common/types (commonv1) for reusable specs
Webhook uses field.ErrorList + apierrors.NewInvalid for structured validation errors
Test file uses table-driven tests with Gomega matching project conventions
Makefile generate/manifests targets are parameterized by operator directory

stories.title: CI pipeline generates and validates CRD artifacts from Go types
stories.want: make generate and make manifests to produce deepcopy code and CRD YAML from Go types
stories.so_that: CRD manifests are always in sync with the Go type definitions
criteria: make generate runs controller-gen object and produces zz_generated.deepcopy.go
make manifests runs controller-gen crd and produces keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml
Generated deepcopy covers all types: Keystone, KeystoneList, KeystoneSpec, KeystoneStatus, FernetSpec, FederationSpec, BootstrapSpec
make build succeeds after generation
make lint passes with generated files excluded via golangci-lint zz_generated path exclusion

requirements.description: The defaulting webhook SHALL set Replicas to 3, Fernet.MaxActiveKeys to 3, Cache.Backend to 'dogpile.cache.pymemcache', Bootstrap.AdminUser to 'admin', and Bootstrap.Region to 'RegionOne' when their values are zero or empty
requirements.rationale: Sensible defaults reduce the configuration burden and match the architecture doc's specified defaults (CC-0011)

scenarios.name: All defaults applied to zero-valued spec
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with replicas=0, empty cache.backend, empty bootstrap.adminUser, empty bootstrap.region, fernet.maxActiveKeys=0
scenarios.then: Default() sets replicas=3, fernet.maxActiveKeys=3, cache.backend='dogpile.cache.pymemcache', bootstrap.adminUser='admin', bootstrap.region='RegionOne'
and_then: the CR is persisted with the defaulted values

scenarios.name: Explicit values not overridden
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with replicas=5, cache.backend='dogpile.cache.redis', bootstrap.adminUser='superadmin', bootstrap.region='US-East-1', fernet.maxActiveKeys=5
scenarios.then: Default() leaves all values unchanged
and_then: the CR is persisted with the originally specified values

scenarios.name: Partial defaults applied
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with replicas=5 but empty cache.backend
scenarios.then: Default() sets only cache.backend='dogpile.cache.pymemcache' and leaves replicas=5

requirements.description: The validation webhook SHALL reject Keystone CRs with replicas less than 1
requirements.rationale: Zero or negative replicas would produce a non-functional deployment; the Kubebuilder marker also enforces Minimum=1 at the schema level

scenarios.name: Replicas of 0 rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with spec.replicas=0 (after defaulting is bypassed or overridden)
scenarios.then: validate() returns a field.Invalid error for spec.replicas with message 'must be at least 1'
and_then: the CR is rejected by the API server

scenarios.name: Replicas of 1 accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with spec.replicas=1
scenarios.then: validate() does not return an error for spec.replicas

scenarios.name: Negative replicas rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with spec.replicas=-1
scenarios.then: validate() returns a field.Invalid error for spec.replicas

requirements.description: The validation webhook SHALL reject Keystone CRs with an invalid cron expression in fernet.rotationSchedule
requirements.rationale: Invalid cron expressions would prevent Fernet key rotation from functioning; robfig/cron/v3 ParseStandard is the authoritative parser

scenarios.name: Valid standard cron accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has fernet.rotationSchedule='0 0 * * 0'
scenarios.then: validate() does not return an error for spec.fernet.rotationSchedule

scenarios.name: Invalid cron expression rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has fernet.rotationSchedule='not-a-cron'
scenarios.then: validate() returns a field.Invalid error for spec.fernet.rotationSchedule containing the cron parse error
and_then: the error message includes the original parse failure from robfig/cron/v3

scenarios.name: Empty cron string rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has fernet.rotationSchedule='' (empty string)
scenarios.then: validate() returns a field.Invalid error for spec.fernet.rotationSchedule

requirements.description: The validation webhook SHALL reject Keystone CRs with duplicate plugin configSection values
requirements.rationale: Duplicate INI section names would cause configuration conflicts; each plugin must have a unique section

scenarios.name: Duplicate configSection rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has two plugins with configSection='keycloak'
scenarios.then: validate() returns a field.Duplicate error for spec.plugins[1].configSection with value 'keycloak'

scenarios.name: Unique configSections accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has plugins with configSection='keycloak' and configSection='ldap'
scenarios.then: validate() does not return an error for spec.plugins

scenarios.name: Empty plugins list accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has no plugins defined
scenarios.then: validate() does not return an error for spec.plugins

requirements.description: The validation webhook SHALL reject PolicyOverrides when neither rules nor configMapRef is set
requirements.rationale: A PolicyOverrides block with no source is a configuration error; the user intended to set policy but provided no content

scenarios.name: PolicyOverrides with nil rules and nil configMapRef rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has policyOverrides set but both rules and configMapRef are nil
scenarios.then: validate() returns a field.Required error for spec.policyOverrides with message 'at least one of rules or configMapRef must be set'

scenarios.name: PolicyOverrides with only rules accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has policyOverrides with rules={'compute:create': 'role:admin'} and nil configMapRef
scenarios.then: validate() does not return an error for spec.policyOverrides

scenarios.name: PolicyOverrides with only configMapRef accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has policyOverrides with nil rules and configMapRef={name: 'my-policy'}
scenarios.then: validate() does not return an error for spec.policyOverrides

requirements.description: The validation webhook SHALL reject PolicyOverrides containing empty rule names
requirements.rationale: Empty rule names are invalid oslo.policy identifiers and would cause runtime errors in OpenStack services

scenarios.name: Empty rule name rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has policyOverrides.rules with an empty string key
scenarios.then: validate() returns a field.Invalid error for spec.policyOverrides.rules with message 'rule name must not be empty'

scenarios.name: Non-empty rule names accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has policyOverrides.rules with valid keys like 'identity:create_project'
scenarios.then: validate() does not return an error for spec.policyOverrides.rules

scenarios.name: Multiple validation errors accumulated
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has both an empty rule name and a duplicate plugin configSection
scenarios.then: validate() returns multiple errors in the field.ErrorList
and_then: all errors are reported together, not just the first one

requirements.description: The KeystoneSpec SHALL include XValidation CEL rules for Database (clusterRef XOR host) and PolicyOverrides (rules OR configMapRef)
requirements.rationale: CEL rules provide API server-level cross-field validation before webhooks, giving the fastest feedback for structural constraints

scenarios.name: Database CEL rule present in CRD YAML
scenarios.when: controller-gen generates the CRD YAML from keystone_types.go
scenarios.then: the Database field contains an x-kubernetes-validations rule with expression 'has(self.clusterRef) != has(self.host)'

scenarios.name: PolicyOverrides CEL rule present in CRD YAML
scenarios.when: controller-gen generates the CRD YAML from keystone_types.go
scenarios.then: the PolicyOverrides field contains an x-kubernetes-validations rule with expression 'self.rules != null || self.configMapRef != null'

scenarios.name: Kubebuilder marker syntax correct
scenarios.when: the XValidation markers are parsed by controller-gen
scenarios.then: generation completes without errors and the CEL expressions are valid

requirements.description: The Keystone CRD types SHALL define all 11 KeystoneSpec fields with correct Go types, JSON tags, and Kubebuilder markers
requirements.rationale: The CRD types are the single source of truth for the Keystone API; all 11 fields specified in the architecture doc must be present

scenarios.name: All fields present with correct types
scenarios.when: the keystone_types.go file is compiled
scenarios.then: KeystoneSpec contains: Replicas (int32), Image (ImageSpec), Database (DatabaseSpec), Cache (CacheSpec), Fernet (FernetSpec), Federation (*FederationSpec), Bootstrap (BootstrapSpec), Middleware ([]MiddlewareSpec), Plugins ([]PluginSpec), PolicyOverrides (*PolicySpec), ExtraConfig (map[string]map[string]string)
and_then: all fields have correct json tags including omitempty where appropriate

scenarios.name: Root type has correct markers
scenarios.when: controller-gen processes keystone_types.go
scenarios.then: Keystone type has +kubebuilder:object:root=true, +kubebuilder:subresource:status, and 3 printcolumn markers (Ready, Endpoint, Age)

scenarios.name: Sub-specs defined correctly
scenarios.when: the types are compiled
scenarios.then: FernetSpec has RotationSchedule (string) and MaxActiveKeys (int32); FederationSpec has Enabled (bool); BootstrapSpec has AdminUser (string), AdminPasswordSecretRef (SecretRefSpec), Region (string)

requirements.description: The controller-gen generation SHALL produce valid deepcopy code and CRD YAML from the Go types
requirements.rationale: Generated artifacts must be in sync with Go types; deepcopy is required for the runtime.Object interface, CRD YAML is required for cluster installation

scenarios.name: DeepCopy generated for all types
scenarios.when: controller-gen object runs on the api/v1alpha1 package
scenarios.then: zz_generated.deepcopy.go is created with DeepCopyObject and DeepCopyInto for Keystone, KeystoneList, KeystoneSpec, KeystoneStatus, FernetSpec, FederationSpec, BootstrapSpec
and_then: the generated code compiles without errors

scenarios.name: CRD YAML generated with full schema
scenarios.when: controller-gen crd runs on the api/v1alpha1 package
scenarios.then: config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml is created with OpenAPI v3 schema, XValidation rules, printer columns, and status subresource

scenarios.name: Makefile targets produce artifacts
scenarios.when: make generate and make manifests are run
scenarios.then: both targets complete successfully and produce the expected files

requirements.description: The Keystone operator main.go SHALL register the Keystone API types with the runtime scheme
requirements.rationale: Without scheme registration, the controller-runtime manager cannot deserialize or watch Keystone CRs

scenarios.name: AddToScheme registered in init()
scenarios.when: the keystone operator main.go init() function runs
scenarios.then: keystonev1alpha1.AddToScheme is called with the scheme, registering Keystone and KeystoneList types
and_then: the +kubebuilder:scaffold:scheme marker is preserved for future additions

scenarios.name: Build succeeds with scheme registration
scenarios.when: make build OPERATOR=keystone runs
scenarios.then: the keystone operator binary compiles successfully

scenarios.name: Import path correct
scenarios.when: main.go imports the v1alpha1 package
scenarios.then: the import uses keystonev1alpha1 alias for github.com/c5c3/forge/operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1

tasks.title: Write reference documentation for Keystone CRD API types and webhooks (CC-0011)
tasks.description: Create `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md` following the existing reference doc pattern in docs/reference/. Content: API version and group, KeystoneSpec field table (name, type, required, default, description for all 11 fields), sub-spec tables (FernetSpec, FederationSpec, BootstrapSpec), KeystoneStatus fields, defaulting webhook behavior (5 defaults with before/after values), validation webhook rules (5 rules with error messages), XValidation CEL rules (2 rules), printer columns (Ready, Endpoint, Age), generation commands (make generate, make manifests). Reference architecture/docs/09-implementation/03-crd-implementation.md for authoritative patterns.

test_specifications.story: Platform operator creates a Keystone CR with minimal spec and gets sensible defaults
test_specifications.expected: Default() should set all 5 fields (replicas, maxActiveKeys, cache.backend, adminUser, region) to their default values when the spec has zero/empty values

test_specifications.story: Platform operator creates a Keystone CR with minimal spec and gets sensible defaults
test_specifications.expected: Default() should not modify any field that already has a non-zero/non-empty value

test_specifications.story: Platform operator creates a Keystone CR with minimal spec and gets sensible defaults
test_specifications.expected: Default() should set each individual default independently (table-driven test per field)

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: validate() should return field.Invalid error for spec.replicas when replicas=0 or negative

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: validate() should not return errors for spec.replicas when replicas=1 or greater

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: validate() should not return errors for standard cron expressions like '0 0 * * 0'

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: validate() should return field.Invalid for spec.fernet.rotationSchedule with 'not-a-cron' and include parse error details

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: validate() should return field.Invalid for spec.fernet.rotationSchedule when empty string

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: validate() should return field.Duplicate for spec.plugins[i].configSection when two plugins share the same section name

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: validate() should not return errors when plugins have distinct configSection values

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: validate() should return field.Required for spec.policyOverrides when both rules and configMapRef are nil

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: validate() should not return errors when policyOverrides has rules but no configMapRef

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: validate() should not return errors when policyOverrides has configMapRef but no rules

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: validate() should return field.Invalid for spec.policyOverrides.rules when a rule key is empty string

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: validate() should return all validation errors (e.g., invalid replicas AND invalid cron) in a single ErrorList, not short-circuit on first error

test_specifications.story: Platform operator creates a Keystone CR with minimal spec and gets sensible defaults
test_specifications.expected: validate() should return no errors for a fully valid Keystone spec with all required fields set correctly

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: ValidateCreate() should call validate() and return its results (no additional create-specific validation)

test_specifications.story: Platform operator gets immediate validation feedback for invalid Keystone specs
test_specifications.expected: ValidateUpdate() should call validate() and return its results (no additional update-specific validation)

review_criteria: All Go files compile without errors: `make build` passes for keystone operator
All unit tests pass: `make test OPERATOR=keystone` exits 0 with all webhook tests green
Lint passes: `make lint OPERATOR=keystone` exits 0 (golangci-lint with errcheck, gocritic, govet, ineffassign, staticcheck, unused)
Generated files are in sync: `make generate` and `make manifests` produce no diff against committed files
CRD YAML contains all expected schema elements: 11 spec fields, 2 XValidation rules, 3 printer columns, status subresource
Test coverage: every defaulting rule (5) and validation rule (5) has at least 2 test cases (positive + negative)
Types follow architecture doc: keystone_types.go matches the reference code in architecture/docs/09-implementation/03-crd-implementation.md
Webhook follows architecture doc: keystone_webhook.go matches the reference code in the architecture doc
Shared types reused: KeystoneSpec imports ImageSpec, DatabaseSpec, CacheSpec, SecretRefSpec, PolicySpec, PluginSpec, MiddlewareSpec from commonv1 (not redefined)
Reference documentation in docs/reference/keystone-crd.md covers all spec fields, defaults, validation rules, and generation commands
implementation_notes:
**Architecture**: Keystone CRD is the reference implementation for all CobaltCore service operators. The pattern established here (groupversion_info.go → types.go → webhook.go → tests → main.go scheme registration → Makefile generation) will be replicated for Nova, Neutron, Glance, etc.

**Shared types**: All reusable specs (ImageSpec, DatabaseSpec, CacheSpec, SecretRefSpec, PolicySpec, PluginSpec, MiddlewareSpec) already exist in `internal/common/types/types.go` (CC-0004). KeystoneSpec imports these as `commonv1`. Only Keystone-specific sub-specs (FernetSpec, FederationSpec, BootstrapSpec) are defined in keystone_types.go.

**Webhook pattern**: Default() uses conditional checks (==0 / =="") rather than always-set to allow Kubebuilder +default markers to also provide schema-level defaults. validate() accumulates all errors in field.ErrorList and returns a single apierrors.NewInvalid — it does NOT short-circuit on the first error. ValidateCreate/ValidateUpdate both delegate to validate() since there are no create-specific or update-specific rules.

**Cron validation**: Uses `github.com/robfig/cron/v3` ParseStandard (5-field cron, no seconds). This package is already an indirect dependency in internal/common/go.mod; adding it as a direct dep in keystone/go.mod.

**Test pattern**: Table-driven tests with `testing.T` + `gomega.NewGomegaWithT(t)`, matching the convention in internal/common/conditions/conditions_test.go and internal/common/plugins/plugins_test.go. In-package tests (package v1alpha1, not v1alpha1_test) for access to unexported validate() method.

**Generation**: controller-gen is used for two purposes: (1) `object` generates DeepCopy methods in zz_generated.deepcopy.go, (2) `crd` generates the full CRD YAML. The Makefile targets are parameterized to scope generation to operators/keystone/api/... paths. golangci-lint already excludes zz_generated.*.go via .golangci.yml.

**Potential pitfalls**: (1) Ensure `api/v1alpha1/` directory is created before writing files. (2) The XValidation CEL expressions must use the exact syntax: `has(self.clusterRef) != has(self.host)` not `self.clusterRef != nil`. (3) PolicyOverrides is a pointer (*PolicySpec) so the validate() function must nil-check before accessing fields. (4) Federation is also a pointer (*FederationSpec) for the optional pattern. (5) The SchemeBuilder.Register call in init() of keystone_types.go must use &Keystone{} and &KeystoneList{}.
